name: disclosure-container
role: Outer container that groups the trigger with its controlled content. Carries the data-state attribute reflecting expanded vs collapsed. Optional element — when omitted, the trigger and content can sit as siblings in any parent.

state.note: Mirrors aria-expanded on the trigger. Operators wire data-state and aria-expanded together via JS so CSS selectors can target either.

ariaPatterns.noRole: disclosure-container has no role of its own. The semantic affordance lives on the trigger + content pair.
ariaPatterns.noLandmark: Containers in a list of related disclosures (accordions) typically sit inside a <ul> or <div role='region'>; the disclosure-container itself stays semantic-neutral.

name: disclosure-trigger
role: The button that toggles content visibility. Always a <button type='button'> (never a link or div). Carries aria-expanded reflecting current state and aria-controls referencing the content's id.

expanded.note: Mirrors the actual content visibility. Updated programmatically when the operator toggles. CSS [aria-expanded='true'] selectors style the open state.

ariaPatterns.expanded: Required. aria-expanded='true' when content is visible; aria-expanded='false' when collapsed. AT users hear the state with the label.
ariaPatterns.controls: Required. aria-controls='<content-id>' associates the trigger with the content it toggles.
ariaPatterns.noToggleRole: role='button' is implicit on <button>. Do NOT add role='button'; do NOT use a non-button element.

keyboardContract.EnterAndSpace: Activate the toggle. Native <button> handles this without JS; the operator's JS need only flip the state.
keyboardContract.Tab: Standard tab order; the trigger sits in DOM order.

name: disclosure-content
role: The content region toggled by the trigger. Uses the HTML `hidden` attribute (or `[hidden] { display: none }`) for the collapsed state. The `id` is referenced by the trigger's aria-controls.

meta.hiddenAttributeContract: The HTML `hidden` attribute (alone) removes the element from layout AND accessibility tree. Use `hidden='until-found'` for find-in-page reveals where appropriate (Baseline 2024).

ariaPatterns.noRoleRegion: role='region' is sometimes added to large content panels (full-page collapsible sections); avoid for small disclosures because region-flood is its own AT problem.
